title: Second largest
prompt_md:
Write `second_largest(nums)` that returns the **second largest** value in a list of numbers.

For example, `second_largest([3, 1, 2])` returns `2`. Duplicates count as the same value, so `second_largest([5, 5, 3])` returns `3`.

You may assume the list has at least two distinct values.
hints: First get the unique values. set(nums) removes duplicates.
Sort the unique values (sorted(...) returns a list) and pick the second-from-last item with index [-2].
